content.full_text:
March 5, 1986

Laurence Gill
Clerk of the Supreme Court
Supreme Court of California
4250 State Building
San Francisco, CA 94102

Re: FPPC Advice No. A-86-077

Dear Mr. Gill:

This letter is a follow-up to our conversation and to your
letter dated February 18, 1986. You asked if an official who has
an interest in a limited partnership is required to report on his
or her statement of Economic Interests the interests held by the
general partner or other limited partners. The official is
required to report only his or her investment interests and is
not required to report the name(s) of the general partner or
limited partner or their financial interests.

If you have any additional questions, please feel free to
contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Alice Hughes
Technical Assistance & Analysis

AH:kt

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

JOHN C. ROSSI
CHIEF DEPUTY

OFFICES

SAN FRANCISCO

ROBERT F. JOHNSON
CHIEF DEPUTY

SAN FRANCISCO 94102
4250 STATE BUILDING
(415) 557-0587

OFFICE OF THE CLERK

LOS ANGELES

FEB 20 8 05 AM

LOS ANGELES 90010
3880 WILSHIRE BLVD.
(213) 736-2902

Supreme Court of California

SACRAMENTO 95814
100 LIBRARY AND COURTS BUILDING
(916) 322-5957

SAN FRANCISCO, CALIFORNIA
LAURENCE P. GILL, CLERK

February 18, 1986

Alice Hughes
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA  95804


Dear Ms. Hughes:

     Please confirm my understanding that one who has
an interest in a limited partnership consisting of a
general partner and three limited partners, need only
report the interest held and need not report the interest
of the general partner or the limited partners.


                                        Very truly yours,

                                        [signature]
                                        LAURENCE P. GILL
                                        Clerk of the Supreme Court

LPG:jrm

sections.question: You asked if an official who has an interest in a limited partnership is required to report on his or her Statement of Economic Interests the interests held by the general partner or other limited partners.
sections.conclusion: The official is required to report only his or her own investment interests and is not required to report the name(s) of the general partner or limited partner or their financial interests.
sections.question_synthetic: Must an official disclose the financial interests of general partners and other limited partners when reporting their own limited partnership interest on the Statement of Economic Interests?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: An official need only report their own limited partnership investment interest and is not required to report the names or financial interests of the general partner or other limited partners.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document includes both incoming request (dated February 18, 1986) and FPPC response (dated March 5, 1986). Clear question and conclusion sections present. Minor OCR issues in footer text but do not affect substantive content extraction.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: You asked if an official who has an interest in a limited partnership is required to report on his or her Statement of Economic Interests the interests held by the general partner or other limited partners.

CONCLUSION: The official is required to report only his or her own investment interests and is not required to report the name(s) of the general partner or limited partner or their financial interests.

embedding.summary: FPPC Advice Letter A-86-077 clarifies that officials with limited partnership interests must report only their own investment interests on the Statement of Economic Interests, not the interests of general or other limited partners. The letter is a response to Clerk of the Supreme Court Laurence Gill's inquiry regarding disclosure requirements for limited partnership holdings.
